description: Figure 12 presents comprehensive TEM characterization of the Fe-35Mn-17Al-5Cu (17Al) alloy aged at 575°C for 6 hours. Panel (a) shows low-magnification bright-field (BF) images revealing the distribution of precipitates within the matrix. Panel (b) provides high-magnification BF imaging together with EDS elemental mapping (Fe, Mn, Al, Cu), demonstrating the elemental distribution between phases, and a selected area electron diffraction (SAED) pattern from the highlighted region confirming FCC twins in the Cu-rich phase. Panel (c) displays BF images of β-Mn precipitates with corresponding SAED pattern along the [111] beam direction, indexing the β-Mn crystal structure, alongside high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) images showing the atomic lattice. Panel (d) presents HRTEM imaging of the interface between the FCC Cu-rich phase and β-Mn, with Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) patterns from both regions confirming their crystallographic structures. The figure reveals the coexistence of hard β-Mn phase and FCC Cu-rich precipitates containing twins, supporting the mechanism of Cu rejection from β-Mn during aging and the formation of secondary phases at interfaces.
